mechanism: Compares cognitive performance across conditions by measuring recall of studied material. No computational method; empirical psychology study.
Records event-related potentials during a stereotype-inhibition task and correlates neural activity with behavioral performance. No novel computational method; analysis is correlational.
Manipulates neural activation via neurofeedback or cognitive task and measures resulting attitudinal changes. No computational method; empirical neuroscience study.
Empirically tests behavioral outcomes across bargaining scenarios with different emotional communications. No computational method; purely experimental.
Presents a descriptive mediation model linking expressed vulnerability to doubts about partner authenticity to rejection perception. No computational method; purely conceptual-empirical.
Tests whether self-reported felt responsiveness predicts perceived partner responsiveness and downstream relationship behaviors. No computational method; correlational observational design.
Measures emotional responses under conditions of anticipated endings versus control. No computational method; purely observational emotional assessment.

Decomposes reaction-time time series via Fourier transform into sinusoidal frequency components; computes power spectral density across frequency bins; inverse relationship between amplitude and frequency (1/f noise) is quantified and compared across effort conditions. 1/f magnitude decreases under high effort. Operates on raw latency observations from implicit bias tasks.
domain: signal analysis; cognitive effort; implicit bias

mechanism: Empirically tests whether global versus local processing styles induce assimilation or contrast in subsequent social judgments. No computational method; behavioral study with inferred processing styles.

Tests whether perceiving personal capability for a wrongdoing predicts forgiveness toward transgressors; identifies mediating factors through correlational and experimental designs. No computational method; behavioral psychology study.

Tests whether facial and body expressions of pride are recognized cross-culturally by having participants from different cultures judge photographs. No computational method; behavioral recognition study.
Correlates performance on cognitive ability tests with propensity to commit various heuristic and biases errors. No novel computational method; correlational analysis of existing data.

Empirically tests whether considering a persuasive message in light of important values increases processing depth and message-attitude relationship strength. No computational method; behavioral design.

Compares willingness to express opinions across three group deviation types (descriptive, prescriptive, nondeviant) in behavioral and observational settings. No computational method; purely empirical comparison.
Tests whether emphasizing past progress versus remaining work affects goal adherence under high versus low commitment. No computational method; experimental design with behavioral outcomes.

Tests whether an implemental (action-planning) mind-set produces stronger, more accessible, less ambivalent attitudes; correlates planning with behavioral prediction. No computational method; behavioral experiments.
Proposes and empirically tests whether restoring victim power and perpetrator moral image promotes reconciliation in conflict. No computational method; purely conceptual-empirical model.
Manipulates information quantity available after a decision and measures preference for decision-consistent versus inconsistent information. No computational method; behavioral experiments.
Manipulates self-regulation resource depletion and measures confirmatory bias in information search and evaluation. No computational method; behavioral experiments with mediational analysis.

Manipulates stereotype threat in anticipated interracial interactions and measures physical distancing behavior; correlates with stereotype activation and implicit prejudice measures. No computational method; behavioral observational study.
